mission.title: kyverno: [Bug] [CLI] Fails offline for CEL/ValidatingPolicy when resources include CRDs
mission.description: [Bug] [CLI] Fails offline for CEL/ValidatingPolicy when resources include CRDs. This issue affects 12+ users.

steps.title: Identify kyverno troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kyverno deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
We run `kyverno apply` in offline mode against rendered manifests (YAML file) as part of a CI linter.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug] [CLI] Fails offline for CEL/ValidatingPolicy when…
steps.description:
*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

Most of the logic introduced by #13565 was already capably of handling multiple CRDs,
```yaml
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  labels:
    argocd.argoproj.io/instance: test-staging
  name: mtest-secrets
  namespace: test
spec:
  dataFrom:
  - extract:
      key: /test
  refreshInterval: 5m
  secretStoreRef:
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
    name: ssm-secret-store
  target:
    name: test-secrets
    template:
      metadata:
        annotations:
          konghq.com/basic-auth-credential: "true"
        labels:
          konghq.com/credential: basic-auth
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug] [CLI] Fails offline for… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

Most of the logic introduced by #13565 was already capably of handling multiple CRDs, but the YAML parser wasn't.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  labels:
    argocd.argoproj.io/instance: test-staging
  name: mtest-secrets
  namespace: test
spec:
  dataFrom:
  - extract:
      key: /test
  refreshInterval: 5m
  secretStoreRef:
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
    name: ssm-secret-store
  target:
    name: test-secrets
    template:
      metadata:
        annotations:
          konghq.com/basic-auth-credential: "true"
        labels:
          konghq.com/credential: basic-auth

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-requests-limits
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources
      requested and consumed by each pod. It is recommended to require 'resources.requests'
      and 'resources.limits.memory' per pod. If a namespace level request or limit is specified,
      defaults will automatically be applied to each pod based on the 'LimitRange' configuration.
spec:
  failurePolicy: Ignore
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - daemonsets

╰─ kyverno apply ./policies/require-requests-limits.yaml --resource=helm-manifest.yaml --detailed-results                                                                                                                ─╯

Applying 1 policy rule(s) to 1 resource(s)...
Error: failed to apply policies on resource mtest-secrets (failed to map gvk to gvr external-secrets.io/v1, Kind=ExternalSecret (no matches for kind "ExternalSecret" in version "external-secrets.io/v1")
)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
